conbody.p: Overview: The No Response Encounter Details dashboard enables CDI Managers/Directors to analyze the encounters with financial impact clarifications that were not responded and view the missed dollar amount associated with it and work with providers to improve the communication with CDSs and overall CDI program adoption

The report provides answers to the following questions.
What is the overall potential missed reimbursement?
How many encounters had at least one no response impact clarification?
What is the potential missed reimbursement for a specific provider?
What is the potential missed reimbursement for Medicare cases?
What are the clarification details of the impact clarifications that were not responded?
Which providers are not responding to the clarifications sent by the CDS team?
Which diagnosis codes are most frequently resulting in 'No Response' impact clarifications?
Which providers should I target to help improve the CDS and Provider communication process and CDI program adoption?
Navigation: On the Analytics home page left side panel, navigate to CDE One Executive Summary No Response Encounter Details .

No Response Encounter Details displays the following:
KPI Cards: Enc with No Response Impact Clarifications, Not Responded Impact Clarifications, Non-Responding Providers, No Response Potential CMI Shift, and Overall No Response Impact.
No Response Encounter Details Tabular Chart
No Response Clarification and Instance Details chart
On the top panel, you can select desired values for the slicers (refer ) and the corresponding data gets generated in the tabular chart.
For filters on all pages, refer .

p.b: No Response Encounter Details - KPI Cards

row.entry: Enc with No Response Impact Clarifications

entry.p: Displays the count of encounters with at least one Financial Impact clarification with Final Response as No Response for the selected time period.
Clarification with a standard or custom ‘Primary Type at Reconciliation’ defined as ‘Financial’ in the admin configuration is a Financial Impact Clarification.
Clarifications with Status 'REMOVED' or 'RETRACTED' are excluded from the count.
Clarifications for "Review Not Needed" encounter status should not be considered for this metric calculation.
Clarifications that do not have a FINAL response are not considered in this calculation.
Displays the count and percentage of Financial Impact Clarifications that were not responded by the providers and with Final Response as No Response for the selected time period.
Format: Not Responded Impact Clarifications (No Response Rate). E.g.12 (56%).
Not Responded Impact Clarifications = Count of Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as “No Response” for the selected time period.
No Response Rate = Out of total Financial Impact clarifications sent, the percentage of Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as “No Response” .
No Response Rate = (Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as No Response / Count of Financial Impact Clarifications Sent) *100.
Displays the count of sent to providers with at least one financial impact clarification instance responded as No Response, i.e. Provider Response Field = No Response.
Clarification with a standard or custom ‘Primary Type at Reconciliation’ defined as ‘Financial’ in the admin configuration is a Financial Impact Clarification.
Clarifications with Status 'REMOVED' or 'RETRACTED' are excluded from the count.
Clarifications for "Review Not Needed" encounter status should not be considered for this metric calculation.
Clarifications that do not have a FINAL response are not considered in this calculation.
Displays the Potential CMI shift and percentage for the financial impact clarifications that were not responded by the providers for the selected time period.
Format: No Response Potential CMI Shift (Potential CMI Shift % ). E.g. 1.4532 (1.67%).
Displays the overall potential dollar amount associated with the financial impact clarifications that the providers did not respond for the selected time period.
Overall No Response Impact = $(No response Potential CMI Shift * Blended Rate).
Format: $4512.

conbody.p: This chart displays a tabular detailed encounter view of all the Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as No Response.
conbody.note: The values of this chart display the regrouped R-DRG related values after code shuffling is completed to move the No Response codes from the last position to secondary position as part of regrouping: R-DRG (For No Response), R-DRG WT (For No Response), R-SOI (For No Response), R-ROM (For No Response), R-AMLOS (For No Response), R-GMLOS (For No Response).

row.entry: Displays the facility assigned to the encounter in the format Facility Description (Facility ID).
The Account Number assigned to the encounter.

The MRN assigned to the encounter.
Example: MRN2021001.

The name of the patient associated with the encounter.
Example: Last Name, First Name.

entry.p: The Admission Date and Time assigned to the encounter in the facility's timezone.
The Discharge Date and Time assigned to the encounter in the facility's timezone.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a discharge date assigned.

row.entry:
The length of stay in days for each encounter.
LOS value is displayed in blue font, when LOS is greater than GMLOS for the encounter.
W-GMLOS value is considered, if F-GMLOS value is blank.

entry.p: Displays the CDS assigned to the encounter in the format Last Name, First Name MI (CDS ID).
Format: Last Name, First Name MI (CDS ID).
The date and time when the initial review was performed on the encounter.
The date is displayed in the Facility's time-zone.

p.note: If the Initial Review Date/time is after the Discharge Date/Time, Initial Review Date/Time is displayed in the red font.

entry.p: The name of the CDS who performed the initial review in the format Last Name, First Name MI (CDS ID).
Example: Ryan, Melissa (rmelissa)
The Tracking Codes assigned to the encounter in comma separated format. You can view a maximum of 10 tracking codes for an encounter.
Example: Quality Review, Prov Education.
The rank assigned to the encounter prior to the Initial Review.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Rank at Initial Review' assigned.
Example: 1, 2, etc.
The count of Financial Impact clarifications with the status other than DELETED or RETRACTED sent for the encounter. Example: 2.
Clarification with a standard or custom ‘Primary Type at Reconciliation’ defined as ‘Financial’ in the admin configuration is a Financial Impact Clarification.
Clarifications with Status 'DELETED' or 'RETRACTED' are excluded from the count.
Clarifications for "Review Not Needed" encounter status are not considered for this metric calculation.

row.entry: Displays the subject of the financial impact clarifications with Final Response as No Response.
Displays the Primary Rec Type of the Financial Impact clarification with Final Response as No Response.
Financial Impact Clarifications with response Agreed, Alternative Diagnosis, Other Explanation

entry.p: The count of sent Financial Impact clarifications with Final response as Agreed, Alternative Diagnosis, and Other Explanation. E.g. 2.
Clarification with a standard or custom ‘Primary Type at Reconciliation’ defined as ‘Financial’ in the admin configuration is a Financial Impact Clarification.
Clarifications with Status 'DELETED' or 'RETRACTED' are excluded from the count.
Clarifications for "Review Not Needed" encounter status are not considered for this metric calculation.
Clarifications that do not have a FINAL response are not considered in this calculation.
The 'primary' Working DRG along with the description displayed on the Review page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC).
The WT associated with the 'primary' Working DRG displayed on the Review page. Example: 1.0393.
The 'SOI' from the working review. Example: 2.

p.note: If the SOI associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the SOI associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: The 'ROM' from the working review. Example: 3.

p.note: If the ROM associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the ROM associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: The 'AMLOS' from the working review. Example: 3.1.

p.note: If the AMLOS associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the AMLOS associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: The 'GMLOS ' from the working review. Example: 3.2.

p.note: If the GMLOS associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the GMLOS associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: The 'primary' Possible DRG along with the description displayed on the Review page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC).

p.note: This column displays the ‘Working DRG’ value in the orange font for the encounters that have a ‘Working DRG’ assigned if the ‘Possible DRG’ is blank or null.

entry.p: The WT associated with the 'primary' Possible DRG displayed on the Review page. Example: 1.0393.

p.note: This column displays the ‘Working DRG WT’ value in the orange font for the encounters that have a ‘Working DRG’ assigned if the ‘Possible DRG’ is blank or null.

entry.p: This column displays the 'SOI' from the possible review. E.g. 2.

p.note: If the SOI associated with the primary possible DRG is not available, then the SOI associated with the advanced possible DRG is displayed.

entry.p: his column displays the 'ROM' from the possible review. E.g. 3.

p.note: If the ROM associated with the primary possible DRG is not available, then the ROM associated with the advanced possible DRG is displayed.

entry.p: Displays the 'AMLOS' from the possible review. E.g. 3.1

p.note: If the AMLOS associated with the primary possible DRG is not available, then the AMLOS associated with the advanced possible DRG is displayed.

entry.p: Displays the 'GMLOS ' from the possible review. E.g. 3.2.

p.note: If the GMLOS associated with the primary possible DRG is not available, then the GMLOS associated with the advanced possible DRG is displayed.

entry.p: Displays both code and description of the Reconciliation Assessment DRG along with the description displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC).
The WT associated with the Reconciliation Assessment DRG displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 1.0393.
The 'SOI' from the Reconciliation Assessment.
Example: 1.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'.

p.note: If the SOI associated with the primary Reconciliation Assessment DRG is not available, then the SOI associated with the advanced Reconciliation Assessment DRG is displayed.

entry.p: The 'ROM' from the Reconciliation Assessment. This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'. Example: 1.

p.note: If the ROM associated with the primary Reconciliation Assessment DRG is not available, then the ROM associated with the advanced Reconciliation Assessment DRG is displayed.

entry.p: The 'AMLOS' from the Reconciliation Assessment. Example: 3.1.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'.

p.note: If the AMLOS associated with the primary Reconciliation Assessment DRG is not available, then the AMLOS associated with the advanced Reconciliation Assessment DRG is displayed.

entry.p: The 'GMLOS' from the Reconciliation Assessment. Example: 2.4.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'.

p.note: If the GMLOS associated with the primary Reconciliation Assessment DRG is not available, then the GMLOS associated with the advanced Reconciliation Assessment DRG is displayed.

entry.p: Displays both code and description of the regrouped (from backend grouping) Reconciliation Assessment DRG along with the description.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC).

p.note: This is displayed in Red font, only for cases with Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as No Response and other Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as Agreed/Alt DX/Other Explanation are present. For other scenarios, display the R-DRG.

entry.p: Displays the WT associated with the regrouped (from backend grouping) Reconciliation Assessment DRG. Example: 1.0393.
entry.note: This is displayed in Red font, only for cases with Financial Impact Clarifications with Final response as No Response and other Financial Impact Clarifications present with Final response as Agreed/Alt DX/Other Explanation. For other scenarios, display the R-DRG WT.

entry.p: Displays the 'SOI' from the regrouped (from backend grouping) Reconciliation Assessment. Example: 1.

p.note: This is displayed in Red font, only for cases with Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as No Response and other Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as Agreed/Alt DX/Other Explanation are present. For other scenarios, display the R-SOI.

entry.p: Displays the 'ROM' from the regrouped (from backend grouping) Reconciliation Assessment. Example: 1

p.note: This is displayed in Red font, only for cases with Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as No Response and other Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as Agreed/Alt DX/Other Explanation are present. For other scenarios, display the R-ROM.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
Displays the 'AMLOS' from the regrouped (from backend grouping) Reconciliation Assessment. Example: 3.1.

p.note: This is displayed in Red font, only for cases with Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as No Response and other Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as Agreed/Alt DX/Other Explanation are present. For other scenarios, display the R-AMLOS.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'.
Displays the 'GMLOS' from the regrouped (from backend grouping) Reconciliation Assessment. Example: 2.4.

p.note: This is displayed in Red font, only for cases with Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as No Response and other Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as Agreed/Alt DX/Other Explanation are present. For other scenarios, display the R-GMLOS.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The code and description of the Final Coded DRG along with the description displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC).
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
Displays the WT associated with the Final Coded DRG displayed on the Final Coded Summary section. Example: 1.0393
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'.
Displays the Final Coded. 'SOI'. Example: 2
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
Advanced SOI Values are used if Primary SOI values are blank
Displays the Final Coded. 'ROM'. Example: 3.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'.
Advanced ROM Values are used if Primary ROM values are blank.
Displays the Final Coded. 'AMLOS'. Example: 3.1.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'.
Advanced AMLOS Values are used if Primary AMLOS values are blank.
Displays the Final Coded. 'GMLOS'. Example: 3.2
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
Advanced GMLOS Values are used if Primary GMLOS values are blank
Displays the shift in the WT at the encounter level.
No Response Encounter Variance = (DRG WT with No Response Code review- DRG WT w/o No Response code review)
Encounter Variance is not calculated for the encounters that do not have an initial review date.
Review Not Needed Encounters are not considered.
Rounded to 4 decimal places. E.g. 1.0178
Displays the Blended Rate associated with the payer/Grouper assigned to the encounter.
The value is displayed in dollars. Example: $7512
When the Blended Rate is not available, the Operating Federal rate is displayed.
Displays the ‘No Response Reimbursement Impact’ value for the encounter.
'Reimbursement Impact' for an encounter is calculated using the below formula:

li.p: Encounter No Response Reimbursement Impact = (Encounter No Response Variance x Blended Rate).
Rounded to 0 decimal places. E.g. $ 5,234.

entry.note: When reconciliation DRG and reconciliation DRG weights for an encounter are matching with your backend grouping reconciliation weights, then there is no financial shift, in that case the 'No Response Variance' is equal to zero and No Response Reimbursement Impact is displayed as $0.

entry.p: Displays the Impact Type assigned to the encounter.

li.p: Financial Impact
SOI/ROM Impact
No Impact
Both Impact

entry.p: Displays the Encounter Status assigned to the encounter.
E.g. Reconciled Impact, Reconciled - No Impact

row.entry: Displays the Payer assigned to the encounter in the format Payer Description (Payer ID).
Displays the Grouper version associated with the payer assigned to the encounter.
Displays the Unit assigned to the encounter.
Displays the Patient Type assigned to the encounter.
Displays the Visit Type assigned to the encounter.

entry.p: Displays the Discharge Status assigned to the encounter in the format 'Discharge Status ID - Discharge Status Description'. E.g. 20 - Expired.

p.b: No Response Clarification and Instance Details Tabular Chart

fig.title: No Response Clarification and Instance Details Chart

conbody.p: This chart displays a clarification and instance level details of all Financial Impact Clarifications instances with Final Response as No Response.

title.b: No Response Clarification and Instance Details Metrics

row.entry: Displays the facility assigned to the encounter in the format Facility Description (Facility ID).
Displays the Account Number assigned to the encounter.

Displays the MRN assigned to the encounter.
Example: MRN2021001.

Displays the name of the patient associated with the encounter.
Example: Last Name, First Name.

entry.p: The Admission Date and Time assigned to the encounter in the facility's timezone.
Displays the Discharge Date and Time assigned to the encounter in the facility's timezone.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a discharge date assigned.
Displays the date and time when the initial review was performed on the encounter.
The date is displayed in the Facility's time-zone.

p.note: If the Initial Review Date/time is after the Discharge Date/Time, Initial Review Date/Time is displayed in the red font.

entry.p: Displays the CDS assigned to the encounter in the format Last Name, First Name MI (CDS ID).
Format: Last Name, First Name MI (CDS ID).

li.p: Displays the name of the CDS that initiated/created the clarification.
Format: Last Name, First Name (CDS ID)
Displays the name of the follow-up CDS assigned to the clarification.
Format: Last Name, First Name (CDS ID).

entry.p: Displays the subject of the financial impact clarification with final response as No Response in the format: Clarification Code Description.
Generic clarifications that do not have code are also displayed.
Example: E871 Hypo-osmolality and hyponatremia

li.p: Displays the sent date and time of the clarification instance.
The date/time is in the Facility's time-zone.
Displays the ‘Sent To’ provider associated with the clarification instance. This is the provider to whom the clarification instance was sent.
Format: Dr. Smith, Dennis B, Jr., MD

entry.p: Displays the response date and time of the clarification instance.
The date/time is in the Facility's time-zone.
Response Date is displayed in red font when the response date/time is after discharge date/time. (Response Date/Time > Discharge Date/Time).

li.p: Displays the Primary Type assigned to the clarification. There can only be one primary type assigned to the clarification. (E.g. PDX, CC, etc.)
A clarification can have a standard or custom type assigned.
Displays the other types assigned to the clarification. Comma-separated values are displayed.
There can be more than one other type selected for the clarification.
A clarification can have standard or custom other types assigned.

row.entry: Displays the primary reconciliation type associated with the clarification. Both Standard and Custom Clarification Types are considered.

li.p: Displays the other reconciliation types assigned to the clarification. Comma-separated values are displayed.
There can be more than one other recon. type selected for the clarification.
A clarification can have standard or custom other types assigned.

entry.p: Displays the status associated with the clarification. (E.g. Sent, Closed, etc.)
Clarifications with the Status ‘REMOVED' , 'RETRACTED' and 'FAILED’ are not displayed.

li.p: Displays the date and time when the clarification was closed.
The date/time is in the Facility's time-zone.
Displays if the clarification was sent electronically.
Displays Y if clarification was sent electronically.
Displays blank if clarification was sent manually.

entry.p: Allows the user to select the discharge date range.
Format: Between Date range, Example: 3/1/2023 - 3/31/2022.
Default: Last 1 calendar months date range selected by default.

ul.li: With the Reset button, you can reset the discharge date range. Use "Ctrl + Click" to reset the Discharge Date.
Displays both active and inactive facilities in alphabetical order that you have access to.
You can select multiple facilities.
You can search the facility by Facility ID or Description.
Format: Facility Description (Facility ID)
Default: ALL.
Displays both active and inactive payers in alphabetical order associated with the facilities you have access to.
You can select multiple payers.
"Unassigned Payer" displays data for the encounters that do not have a payer assigned.
You can search the Payer by Payer ID or Description.
Format: Payer Description (Payer ID)
Default: ALL

li.p: This filter displays the list of available units.
User can select multiple Units.
Users can search the Unit.
Default: ALL.
Displays both active and inactive providers that are sent To provider for at least one instance.
Format: Prefix Lastname, Firstname Middle Initial, Suffix, Title.
Default: ALL.

entry.p: Displays the type (Medical, Surgical, Unknown) associated with the Final MS-DRG Code:
Values:
S- Surgical
M- Medical
U- Unknown

ul.li: Displays the available patient types. Example: Inpatient, Outpatient.
Default: Inpatient
Displays the list of available admit service.
You can select multiple admit services.
You can search the admit service.
Default: ALL
Displays the available patient types. Example: ED, IP, etc.
You can select multiple Visit Type.
You can search the Visit Type.
Default: ALL

li.p: Displays the available DRG Specialties. Example: Transplant, Surgery, Neurosurgery.

ul.li: You can select multiple specialties.
You can search the DRG Specialty.
Default: ALL

row.entry: Out of total Financial Impact clarifications sent, the percentage of Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as “No Response”. No Response Rate = (Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as No Response / Count of Financial Impact Clarifications Sent) *100.

P-DRG: Displays the ‘Working DRG’ value in orange font for the reconciled encounters with blank ‘Possible DRG’. P-DRG WT: Displays the ‘Working DRG WT’ value in orange font for the reconciled encounters with blank ‘Possible DRG’.
Displays Initial Review Date/time in red font when the Initial Review Date/Time > Discharge Date/Time.

R-DRG (For No Response): Displays the regrouped Reconciliation DRG (from backend grouping) along with the description in red font. R-DRG WT (For No Response): Displays the regrouped Reconciliation DRG WT (from backend grouping) along with the description in red font. This is displayed in Red font, only for cases with Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as No Response and other Financial Impact Clarifications with Final Response as Agreed/Alt DX/Other Explanation are present; For other scenarios, display the R-DRG/R-DRG WT details.

entry.p: Displays the shift in the WT at the encounter level.
No Response Encounter Variance = (DRG WT with No Response Code review- DRG WT w/o No Response code review).
Encounter Variance is not calculated for the encounters that do not have an initial review date. Review Not Needed Encounters are not considered. Rounded to 4 decimal places. E.g. 1.0178.
entry.note: When reconciliation DRG and reconciliation DRG weights for an encounter are matching with your backend grouping reconciliation weights, then there is no financial shift, in that case the 'No Response Variance' is equal to zero and No Response Reimbursement Impact is displayed as $0.

row.entry: Configured at payer level. When Blended Rate is not available, Operating Federal rate is displayed.

The sum of No Response Impact ((Potential CMI with No Response Impact Clari - Potential CMI w/o No Response Impact Clari) * Blended Rate) for all Reconciled - No Impact(Impact Type: No Impact) & Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: SOI/ROM, Financial, Both). E.g. $5,234,786.

Clarification with a standard or custom ‘Primary Type at Reconciliation’ defined as ‘Financial’ in the admin configuration. Clarification Status: Includes all clarification statuses except 'Removed' or 'Retracted'.
